Complements mesh_iblt_conformance (decode behaviour) — pins foundational constants + cell-budget formula every gossip node uses. Pinned: IBLT_HASH_COUNT=3 (drift breaks peeling invariant), MIN_RECOMMENDED_IBLT_CELLS=64; recommended_cell_count formula max(MIN, ceil(3*N/2)) — 0→64 floor, 1..40→64 (formula<floor), 43→65 first-above-floor, 100→150, 1000→1500, 10k→15k; saturates on usize::MAX no panic; const fn usable in const contexts; Iblt::default==with_expected_difference(0)→64 cells; with_cell_count rejects 0/1/2 + accepts exactly 3; IbltDecodeResult::default empty+incomplete + is_complete reflects field + PartialEq full; IbltCell::default zero-everything + serde roundtrip; IbltError Display literal substrings + payload values + Copy + PartialEq. 19/19 green in 0.00s. Commit 59d41dde.","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:27:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a8o8z","title":"[REVIEW-P3][HIGH] fcp-host RPC dispatcher still hangs callers when the per-connector queue fills","description":"crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:190-214 moved SubprocessConnector RPCs onto runner_tx, but self.runner_tx.send(...).await has no timeout while crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:706-714 caps the queue at 64 and the 10s timeout only wraps stdin/stdout I/O at crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:772-799. Bug scenario: one connector RPC stalls in the dispatcher task, 64 more requests queue behind it, and the 65th caller blocks indefinitely in send().await instead of failing fast. That is a new adjacent regression in e6f125b3: the old mutex hang became queue-backpressure hang with no caller deadline. Suggested fix: wrap runner_tx.send and response_rx.await in a host-side timeout or queue-timeout error, and add a regression test that queue saturation returns an error rather than hanging.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:25:38.776616Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:08:42.357043Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:08:42.356807Z","close_reason":"Resolved via r36uq dispatcher fix (commit 58e25d0e)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a8xp","title":"TEST: Checkpoint/Frontier Unit Tests (Advancement, Fork Detection, Convergence)","description":"# TEST: Checkpoint/Frontier Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate ZoneCheckpoint/frontier advancement and fork detection semantics with deterministic unit/integration tests.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for checkpoint management and fork detection.

## Test Categories

### Checkpoint Advancement
- [ ] Normal advancement with quorum signatures
- [ ] Advancement blocked without quorum
- [ ] Checkpoint sequence monotonically increases
- [ ] Checkpoint binds AuditHead + RevocationHead
- [ ] Checkpoint signature verification

### Fork Detection
- [ ] Conflicting checkpoints detected
- [ ] Fork evidence is preserved
- [ ] Fork triggers alert/pause
- [ ] Fork resolution protocol
- [ ] No silent fork acceptance

### Convergence
- [ ] Nodes converge on same checkpoint
- [ ] Late-joining node catches up
- [ ] Partition recovery reaches consistency
- [ ] Checkpoint gossip propagation
- [ ] Convergence metrics tracking

### Frontier Management
- [ ] Frontier tracks latest valid checkpoint per zone
- [ ] Stale frontier detection
- [ ] Frontier update atomic with heads

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/checkpoint/`
- `checkpoint_valid.cbor`
- `checkpoint_fork_evidence.cbor`
- `convergence_scenario.json`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_fork_detection",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "zone_id": "z:work",
  "checkpoint_seq": 42,
  "fork_detected": true,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fork detection is reliable
- Convergence is achieved after partition heal
- All checkpoint operations are audit-logged

+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-aaksp","title":"Pin fcp-core RegistrySnapshot Display","description":"Add tests in crates/fcp-core/tests/ pinning RegistrySnapshot Display + serde.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"cod6","created_at":"2026-04-29T08:00:13.767679Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-29T08:12:25.195232Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T08:12:25.194979Z","close_reason":"Pinned fcp-core RegistrySnapshot Display and serde via RegistryEntry; verified with rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-core","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ab8sb","title":"[REVIEW R2 P4][HIGH] fcp-e2e/llm_router: full request handshake still fabricates acceptance","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/tests/llm_router_compliance_e2e.rs:71-100 serializes the real HandshakeRequest and calls handle_handshake(params), but never inspects the connector response; it then synthesizes HandshakeResponse locally with SessionId::new(), a hardcoded manifest_hash, and granted capabilities copied from the request. This bypasses connector handshake semantics and can hide auth/capability negotiation regressions. Fix: parse the connector handshake response and assert the returned contract instead of constructing a local accepted response.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:51:06.522897Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T20:02:49.641801Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T20:02:49.640799Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2846,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ab8sb","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit f7ce2136. Same pattern as c9561961 (spotify), d18041eb (terraform), 9fbcbcb1 (7 more) but adapted for llm-router's response shape: unlike spotify/segment which return an ad-hoc JSON envelope, llm-router serializes the full HandshakeResponse struct back. Adapter now deserializes via serde_json::from_value::<HandshakeResponse> and returns it, with on-the-way assertions: serde failure → named FcpError::Internal; status == 'accepted'; nonce round-trips; no granted capability outside capabilities_requested. Companion hunk added fcp-sandbox to fcp-e2e dev-dependencies (the test file references fcp_sandbox::host_matches_allow_list at line 437; without it the binary did not compile on baseline, blocking ab8sb verification — pre-existing unrelated to ab8sb's handshake logic). Verified: cargo test -p fcp-e2e --test llm_router_compliance_e2e — 3/3 pass. — cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-24T20:02:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-abr7b","title":"Pin fcp-core RequestId Display roundtrip variants","description":"Add tests in crates/fcp-core/tests/ pinning RequestId Display+FromStr roundtrip across construction variants and equality.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"cc1","created_at":"2026-04-28T19:29:22.096185Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:52:39.293766Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T19:52:39.293415Z","close_reason":"Shipped: crates/fcp-core/tests/request_id_display_roundtrip.rs (13/13) pins Display verbatim emission, Display→FromStr round-trip Eq+Hash, equality across new/FromStr/From/clone construction paths, RequestId::random req_<20-digit> format + distinctness, HashMap-key correctness, serde JSON newtype quoted-string form (anchored exactly), empty-string round-trip, pub-field access matches Display. Variants covered: monotonic, user-supplied req_NNN, UUID hyphenated/simple, opaque tokens, mixed-case, single-char, empty.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-abwt","title":"TEST: Canonical Serialization Unit Tests (Deterministic CBOR, Schema Hash, CDDL Validation)","description":"# TEST: Canonical Serialization Unit Tests

## Goal
Prove that serialization is deterministic and schema-bound.

## Test Categories

### Deterministic CBOR Encoding
- [ ] Same logical object → same bytes (always)
- [ ] Map key ordering is canonical (sorted)
- [ ] Integer encoding is minimal
- [ ] Float encoding is canonical
- [ ] No duplicate map keys
- [ ] Definite length encoding used

### Schema Hash Binding
- [ ] SchemaId (namespace, name, version) hashes correctly
- [ ] SchemaHash is 32 bytes (BLAKE3)
- [ ] Payload format: schema_hash || canonical_cbor_bytes
- [ ] Schema hash mismatch detected on decode

### Schema Validation
- [ ] Valid object passes schema check
- [ ] Missing required field rejected
- [ ] Wrong field type rejected
- [ ] Extra fields handled per policy (reject or ignore)
- [ ] Nested object validation works

### CDDL Compliance
- [ ] All FCP2 objects have CDDL definitions
- [ ] Generated bytes match CDDL constraints
- [ ] CDDL can be used for cross-language validation

### Roundtrip Tests
- [ ] Encode → decode → encode produces identical bytes
- [ ] All core object types roundtrip correctly
- [ ] Large objects roundtrip correctly
- [ ] Nested objects roundtrip correctly

### Bounds and Limits
- [ ] Oversized objects rejected at encode time
- [ ] Deeply nested objects bounded
- [ ] String length limits enforced
- [ ] Array length limits enforced

### Decode Safety
- [ ] Malformed CBOR rejected without panic
- [ ] Truncated input rejected
- [ ] Oversized length prefix rejected (DoS prevention)
- [ ] Recursive structure depth bounded

## Golden Vectors
- Each core object type with canonical bytes
- Schema hash computation examples
- Malformed inputs that must be rejected

## Property Tests
- Determinism: same input → same output
- Commutativity with schema: schema_hash(decode(encode(x))) == schema_hash(x)

## Fuzz Targets
- CBOR decode with arbitrary bytes
- Schema validation with malformed objects

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "object_type": "...",
  "schema_id": "...",
  "operation": "encode|decode|validate",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All core types have golden vectors
- Determinism verified by property tests
- Fuzz targets find no panics
